drills.title: All-spin validity: TETR.IO-valid residue shape
drills.goal: Recognize the TETR.IO-valid all-spin residue shelf: a paired S side-tooth (left, lower-then-upper) and a Z side-tooth (right) that keep a one-cell cavity under each overhang. The accepted outcome judges only the final form shape; it does not check that a spin was executed.

variants.label: Paired S + Z residue on the floor

acceptedOutcomes.label: Valid paired S+Z residue
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: S locked flat with its lower row on the floor at columns 0-1 and its upper row stepped up at columns 1-2; Z mirrors this on the right at columns 7-8 (upper) and 8-9 (lower). The gaps under the upper teeth are the all-spin-style cavities. V2 matches the final board cells only.

solutionRoutes.label: S on the left, Z on the right
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop S at engine origin (0, -1): the SRS local grid floats the S one row above the origin, so the lower tooth rests on the floor at columns 0-1 and the upper tooth sits at row 1, columns 1-2. Then hard-drop Z at (7, -1): the lower row rests at columns 8-9 and the upper row rests at columns 7-8. The two cavities under each upper tooth are the recognizable all-spin residue shape.

badTemptations.label: Flat single-row placement
badTemptations.explanation: Laying the pieces flat with no overhang removes the under-tooth cavities. Without a cavity there is no TETR.IO-valid all-spin residue to recognize. Replay of the bad temptation is not modeled in V2; this is a final-form contrast only.

drills.title: All-spin as secondary B2B filler
drills.goal: Recognize how an all-spin residue can act as a secondary filler shape that preserves playable parity for the next attack. The accepted outcome judges only the final-form residue; B2B and attack sequence are not modeled in V2.

variants.label: S filler on the left side
Z filler on the right side

acceptedOutcomes.label: Left S filler residue
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: S nudges left of center with its lower row at columns 1-2 and upper row at columns 2-3. The shape keeps the right side open for the next attack and leaves a one-cell cavity under the upper tooth. Designed for final-form matching only.

acceptedOutcomes.label: Right Z filler residue
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: Z nudges right of center with its lower row at columns 7-8 and upper row at columns 6-7. The shape keeps the left side open for the next attack and leaves a one-cell cavity under the upper tooth. Designed for final-form matching only.

solutionRoutes.label: Place the left S filler
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop S at (1, -1): the lower tooth locks at columns 1-2 (row 0) and the upper tooth sits at columns 2-3 (row 1). This is the SRS local-grid floating offset used by the engine.

solutionRoutes.label: Place the right Z filler
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop Z at (6, -1): the lower row locks at columns 7-8 (row 0) and the upper row sits at columns 6-7 (row 1).

drills.title: All-spin stack-balance shape
drills.goal: Recognize a balanced all-spin residue: the same number of stack-height cells on the left and right of the playfield, produced by mirror-method placements. The accepted outcome judges only the final balanced shape; spin execution is not modeled.

acceptedOutcomes.label: L/J canonical balanced final form
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: L on the left (flat row at columns 0-2 with the bump pointed up at column 2) and a J on the right (flat row at columns 7-9 with the bump pointed up at column 7). The two bumps are mirror positions, so the leftover cavities balance both stack sides. V2 matches only the locked cells.

solutionRoutes.label: L on the left, J mirrored on the right
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop L at (0, -1) for the left bump pointing up at column 2; then hard-drop J at (7, -1) for the right bump pointing up at column 7. The two raised bumps balance the stack.

drills.title: All-spin local-parity repair shape
drills.goal: Recognize the parity-repair S posture that sits over a thin mid-board lip and reopens the column parity affected by a prior placement. The accepted outcome judges only the final repair shape; it does not check that a spin was used.

variants.label: S rests over a mid-board J lip

acceptedOutcomes.label: S over the J lip (parity repaired)
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: with a thin J lip spanning columns 3-7 on row 0, the S piece rests flush on top of the lip with its lower row at columns 4-5 (row 1) and upper row at columns 5-6 (row 2). The lip's central columns block the standard bottom cavity, so the only recognizable 'repair' comes from the S posture choice. The mask constrains only the S cells; the J lip cells are unconstrained so any matching residue still passes. V2 matches only the final board cells.

solutionRoutes.label: S rests flush over the J lip
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop S at engine origin (4, 0). Because columns 4-5 of row 0 are occupied by the J lip, the SRS origin slides up by one so the S lower tooth rests at row 1 (columns 4-5) and the upper tooth at row 2 (columns 5-6).

drills.title: All-spin clean vs dirty aftermath
drills.goal: Recognize a clean all-spin aftermath: a single S shelf flat against a low stack with no extra hanging cell above it. The accepted outcome judges only the clean final shape; it does not detect that an all-spin was performed.

acceptedOutcomes.label: Clean isolated S shelf
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: S with its lower row flat on the floor at columns 4-5 (row 0) and upper row at columns 5-6 (row 1); nothing above the upper row. No 'hangover' cell at row 2 means the aftermath reads as clean. Final-form match only.

solutionRoutes.label: Place the clean S shelf
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop S at (4, -1) for an isolated two-row S shape with no overhang above it.

badTemptations.label: Dirty overhang residue
badTemptations.explanation: Leaving an extra hanging cell above the S shelf clutters stack parity and reads as a dirty aftermath. Replay of the bad temptation is not modeled in V2; this is a final-form contrast only.

drills.title: Scarlet final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize the Scarlet final-form: a low central I prop with S residue on the left and Z residue on the right, the signature shelf layout used by the Scarlet method. V2 judges only the final form; the Scarlet spin sequence is not modeled.

variants.label: I prop flanked by S and Z residues

acceptedOutcomes.label: I prop + S (left) + Z (right)
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: a horizontal I shelf flat on the floor at columns 4-7, S residue on the lower-left at columns 1-2 and upper at columns 2-3, and Z residue on the lower-right at columns 8-9 and upper at columns 7-8. The left S, central I, right Z arrangement is the recognizable Scarlet signature shape. Final-form match only.

solutionRoutes.label: I prop, then S, then Z
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop the I at (4, -1) for the central shelf at columns 4-7; then drop S at (1, -1) and Z at (7, -1) to flank it. The Z's upper row rests at columns 7-8 because column 7 row 0 is already filled by the I prop.

drills.title: Coral final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize the Coral final-form: a flat, broad T anchor at columns 3-5 with an S residue tucked against its left shoulder forming the Coral shelf shape. V2 judges only the final form; the Coral spin sequence is not modeled.

variants.label: T anchor with left S residue

acceptedOutcomes.label: T anchor (center) + S residue (left)
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: T flat across columns 3-5 (row 0) with the Coral bump at column 4 (row 1), and S tucked to the left with its lower row at columns 0-1 (row 0) and upper row at columns 1-2 (row 1). The left S shoulder running flush with the T bump is the recognizable Coral posture. Final-form match only.

solutionRoutes.label: T anchor, then left S
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop T at (3, -1) so the flat row rests at columns 3-5 (row 0) and the bump points up at column 4 (row 1). Then hard-drop S at (0, -1) to lay the left residue flat with its upper tooth at columns 1-2.

drills.title: Amaranth final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize the Amaranth final-form: a left-side Z side-tooth and a right-side J anchor forming the recognizable Amaranth posture. V2 judges only the final form; the Amaranth spin sequence is not modeled.

acceptedOutcomes.label: Z (left) + J anchor (right)
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: Z at columns 4-5 (row 0) and 3-4 (row 1) - the left-side Z side-tooth - paired with a J anchor flat across columns 6-8 (row 0) with the bump at column 6 (row 1). The Z side-tooth tucked against the J bump is the recognizable Amaranth posture. Final-form match only.

solutionRoutes.label: Z left side-tooth, then right J anchor
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop Z at (3, -1): the lower row locks at columns 4-5 and the upper row at columns 3-4. Then hard-drop J at (6, -1) for the right anchor with its bump pointing up at column 6.

drills.title: Kaidan setup/final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize a Kaidan T-slot final form: a T pointing into a one-cell slot under a short shoulder between two stacks. The accepted outcome checks the recognizable final-form T-slot shape, not the spin itself.

acceptedOutcomes.label: Kaidan center T-slot
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: with shoulders at columns 2-3 (J) and 7-8 (L), the T pointing down rests its flat row on row 2 above the pillar at column 5 (row 0). The T nub at column 5, row 1 sits one row above the J pillar that fills the slot. The mask constrains only the T cells; the shoulder/pillar cells are unconstrained so different stack contents still pass. V2 matches only the final board cells.

acceptedOutcomes.label: Kaidan offset T-slot
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: with shoulders at columns 2-4 (J) and 7-8 (L), the T pointing down rests its flat row on row 2 at columns 3-5 with its nub over the pillar at column 4 (row 0). The flat-row offset to the left gives a distinctively different final form from the center variant. V2 matches only the final board cells.

solutionRoutes.label: T pointing down over the center pillar
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop T at (4, 1) in rotation 2: the flat row rests at row 2 cols 4-6 and the nub sinks to column 5, row 1, one row above the J pillar at column 5. The T cannot rest at y=0 because the nub would collide with the column-5 pillar.

solutionRoutes.label: T pointing down over the offset pillar
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop T at (3, 1) in rotation 2: the flat row rests at row 2 cols 3-5 and the nub sinks to column 4, row 1, over the J pillar at column 4.

drills.title: Parapet setup/final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize a Parapet T-slot final form: a T pointing down with its nub sinking into a one-cell gap between left and right low walls. The accepted outcome checks the recognizable final-form parapet shape, not the spin itself.

variants.label: T nub sinks into the parapet gap

acceptedOutcomes.label: Parapet T-slot
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: low walls at columns 2-3 (J) and 5-6 (L) leave a one-cell gap at column 4. The T points down with its nub sinking into the gap at column 4, row 0 and its flat row resting across columns 3-5 on row 1. The nub-in-gap posture is the recognizable Parapet shape. Final-form match only.

solutionRoutes.label: T pointing down into the gap
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop T at (3, 0) in rotation 2: the flat row rests across columns 3-5 on row 1 while the nub sinks into the column 4 gap at row 0. The T cannot rest lower because the wall cells at columns 3 and 5 would collide with the flat row behind.

drills.title: STMB Cave (clean) setup/final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize a clean STMB Cave final form: the parapet T-slot pattern plus a one-cell cave roof directly over the slot, the clean recognizable cave posture. The accepted outcome checks the recognizable shape only; the STMB Cave spin is not modeled.

variants.label: Parapet slot under a one-cell roof

acceptedOutcomes.label: Parapet T-slot with a cave roof
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: the same parapet T-slot (nub in the column-4 gap, flat row across columns 3-5 at row 1) plus a one-cell J roof floating at column 4, row 2 directly above the nub. The roof cell above the slot is the recognizable STMB Cave clean posture. The mask constrains the T cells and the roof cell; the wall cells are unconstrained. Final-form match only.

solutionRoutes.label: Intended final-form placement: T pointing down into the parapet slot under the roof
solutionRoutes.explanation: Intended final-form placement for the recognizable STMB Cave shape: T in rotation 2 with its nub in the column-4 gap and its flat row across columns 3-5 at row 1, sitting under the authored cave roof at column 4, row 2. A real STMB Cave reaches this posture through a spin/soft-drop sequence under the roof; V2 does not simulate spins, hold/soft-drop inputs, or piece motion from spawn. The supplied (3, 0) origin is the authored grounded hard-drop origin used by buildSolutionSteps to validate the final board form only.

drills.title: Sky Prop setup/final-form recognition
drills.goal: Recognize a Sky Prop final form: a T pointing up at the top of a small column-set prop, lifted one row by an overhanging lip on each side. The accepted outcome judges only the recognizable lifted final form; the Sky Prop spin is not modeled in V2.

variants.label: T uplifted one row over the prop

acceptedOutcomes.label: T lifted one row by the prop
acceptedOutcomes.explanation: Final form: a prop of L cells at columns 5-6 (row 0) with overhanging lips at column 4 and column 6 (row 1), and a T pointing up at row 2 (columns 4-6 flat) with its bump at column 5 (row 3). The bump-in-the-air posture is the recognizable lifted Sky Prop shape. V2 matches only the final board cells.

solutionRoutes.label: Hard-drop T up over the lips
solutionRoutes.explanation: Hard-drop T at (4, 1) in rotation 0 (bump pointing up): the flat row rests at row 2 (columns 4-6) and the bump lands at row 3 column 5. The T cannot rest at y=0 because the overhanging lips at row 1 columns 4 and 6 would collide with the flat row.
